mainText:
Good morning. Here is your daily brief for Saturday 23 May 2026.

🤖 AI models and products

- Subquadratic released SubQ 1M-Preview on May 5 with 29 million dollars in seed funding, featuring a 12-million-token native context window, representing a shift toward non-transformer architectures for cost-efficient long-context work.

- Gemini 3.5 Flash achieved general availability with frontier-level intelligence at 4 times the speed of comparable models, priced at 1 dollar 50 per 1 million input tokens, setting a new price-to-performance baseline for agentic workflows.

- Every major AI lab now has its own agent framework: OpenAI launched the Agents SDK, Google released ADK, Anthropic shipped the Agent SDK, and HuggingFace built Smolagents, signalling consolidation around agentic development infrastructure.

💷 UK personal finance

- Trading 212 offers 4.62% on easy access cash ISAs, the top rate for new money, meaning your tax-free savings can match inflation-beating returns.

- The best fixed ISA rates reached 4.70% AER for one year and 4.71% AER for two years as of May 22, locking in strong yields ahead of potential Bank of England hikes later this year.

- The Bank Rate sits at 3.75% after the December 2025 cut, but financial markets expect the Bank to raise rates to 4.25% by end of 2026 due to inflation pressures from the Middle East conflict, which could push your mortgage costs higher.

🇮🇱 Israeli politics

- The Knesset advanced a bill to dissolve itself in May 2026, opening the possibility of snap elections months before the scheduled October 2026 vote.

- Rabbi Dov Lando, the spiritual leader of the Degel HaTorah faction, launched an attack on Prime Minister Netanyahu, labeling him a "liar" and instructing party members to move toward dissolving the Knesset.

- Right-wing former prime minister Naftali Bennett is a front-runner in polls seeking to unseat Netanyahu, running together with centrist opposition head Yair Lapid.

🇬🇧 UK politics

- Labour lost more than 1,100 council seats, but Starmer has rejected calls for his resignation.
- Reform UK took Havering, its first London council, signalling Nigel Farage's hard-right party is now a genuine London force.
- The government has backed a £200 million training programme so every teacher can support children with special educational needs.

😴 Sleep and wearables

- Oura Ring achieves 85 percent accuracy for sleep and wake detection, making it useful for tracking your eight years of Fitbit data patterns.

- Google's new Fitbit Air launches May 26 at $99 without subscriptions, undercutting Whoop and offering better value than Oura Ring pricing.

- Sleep disruption analysis claims 15 percent more accuracy than previous models through Gemini-powered AI, potentially improving your personal sleep experiment insights.

📈 B2B SaaS and go-to-market

- DealHub raised a 100 million dollar growth round in January to advance its agentic quote-to-revenue platform, marking the shift toward AI agents handling complex GTM workflows.

- Nearly half, 46 percent, of US-based B2B go-to-market leaders plan to increase their AI sales tool investment in 2026, driven by need for conversation intelligence and deal forecasting.

- AI companies command roughly a 42 percent premium at seed and about 70 percent at Series A, but investors now demand proof AI drives retention or reduces customer acquisition cost, not just buzzwords.

Today's question: With 46 percent of B2B go-to-market leaders planning to increase AI sales tool investment this year, are you advising your clients on which agent frameworks to standardize on, or are you building proprietary GTM agents yourself to differentiate your advisory?
